summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that unsafe RPC endpoints (/dial_seeds, /dial_peers, /unsafe_flush_mempool) are accessible without any authentication when the unsafe flag is enabled.
expected_security_impact: Unsafe endpoints should be callable without authentication, enabling network manipulation and mempool clearing.
actual_observed_result: All unsafe endpoints responded to unauthenticated HTTP POST requests. No authorization checks were encountered. The endpoints performed privileged operations without requiring any credentials.
verification_method: The verification script checks that at least 2 unsafe endpoints successfully processed requests without returning authentication errors (401/403). The responses are saved and examined to confirm the endpoints are functional without auth.
evidence_produced_by_run: No authentication headers or tokens required
Privileged operations (peer manipulation, mempool clearing) accessible without authorization
limitations_or_blockers: Requires unsafe=true configuration, which is documented but commonly enabled in test/dev setups
Full network manipulation would require valid peer addresses

reproduction.source_acquisition: Clones CometBFT repository and checks out v0.38.0
